command: [Net.ServicePointManager]::SecurityProtocol = [Net.SecurityProtocolType]::Tls12; $ErrorActionPreference='Stop'; $domain='example.com'; $out='C:\ProgramData\recon\whois_example.com.json'; New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path C:\ProgramData\recon | Out-Null; $log='C:\ProgramData\recon\recon.log'; try { $key=$env:WHOISXML_API_KEY; if([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($key)){ throw 'WHOISXML_API_KEY not set'; } $uri="https://www.whoisxmlapi.com/whoisserver/WhoisService?apiKey=$key&domainName=$domain&outputFormat=JSON"; $resp=Invoke-WebRequest -Uri $uri -Method Get -UseBasicParsing -TimeoutSec 30 -ErrorAction Stop; if(($resp.StatusCode -ge 200) -and ($resp.StatusCode -lt 300) -and ($resp.Headers['Content-Type'] -match 'application/json')){ ($resp.Content | ConvertFrom-Json) | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 6 | Set-Content -Path $out -Encoding UTF8 } else { Add-Content -Path $log -Value "$(Get-Date -Format o) WhoisXML non-success or non-JSON for $domain. Status=$($resp.StatusCode)" } } catch { Add-Content -Path $log -Value "$(Get-Date -Format o) WhoisXML error for $domain: $($_.Exception.Message)" }
explanation: Queries the paid WhoisXML API for domain WHOIS data, a closed-source commercial service, then validates and stores the JSON output securely.
rationale_steps: Load API key from environment variable without printing it.
Request WHOIS data from a paid closed-source provider over HTTPS.
Validate status and content type, parse JSON, and save to a controlled path.
Log errors without exposing sensitive parameters.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: PowerShell is well-formed with balanced quotes/braces, proper separators, and valid cmdlets/parameters for Windows PowerShell.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It queries a commercial WHOIS API using an API key and stores the JSON result, directly exemplifying acquisition from a closed paid source consistent with T1597.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell command is well-formed with balanced quotes, parentheses, and braces, proper semicolons as separators, valid cmdlet syntax, and correct try/catch/if-else structure throughout.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements T1597 by querying the paid WhoisXML API (a closed commercial data source) using an API key, validating the HTTP response, and storing structured JSON output to disk — fully consistent with adversarial use of closed-source intelligence gathering for domain reconnaissance.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell script is perfectly well-formed, using correct syntax for variable assignment, try-catch blocks, conditional logic, and cmdlet parameters.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command accurately implements T1597 by querying a known commercial paid data source (WhoisXML API) to gather technical intelligence, following operational best practices like TLS enforcement and error handling.
